title: calocephalus citreus
intro: Calocephalus citreus, commonly known as lemon lovegrass, is a flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and feathery foliage. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Calocephalus citreus include relieving respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as soothing skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
